note: Academic City's Business programmes are fully accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC) and lead to the award of a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) degree.
Business programmes share a common entry requirement: credits in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science plus three elective subjects with an overall WASSCE aggregate of 36 or better.
The Business faculty emphasises entrepreneurship, leadership, and real-world case-based learning, with guest lectures and industry partnerships integrated into each programme.
Graduates are eligible for professional certification pathways including ICAG, ACCA, CIMA, CIM, and CIPD, depending on their specialisation.

Students undertake a compulsory internship in their third year, gaining hands-on exposure in financial institutions and corporate firms.

note: Academic City's Informatics faculty offers both undergraduate BSc degrees and postgraduate Masters programmes, all accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC).
Undergraduate Informatics programmes require credits in English, Mathematics, and Integrated Science plus three relevant elective subjects with a WASSCE aggregate of 36 or better.

Masters programmes require a BSc degree from an accredited university with a minimum Second Class Upper or Second Class Lower (CWA above 50%), with preference given to backgrounds in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering.
All Informatics programmes are delivered in state-of-the-art computing labs with high-speed internet, cloud computing infrastructure, and access to AI and data science toolkits.

Overall aggregate of 36 or better
SSSCE equivalent: aggregate 24 or better
level_note: BSc Artificial Intelligence is one of the most competitively priced and technically advanced AI undergraduate programmes in West Africa.
Students have access to GPU computing clusters, AI research labs, and partnerships with global technology companies for internships and live project briefs.

level_note: MSc Cyber Security at Academic City is one of the few dedicated postgraduate cybersecurity programmes in Ghana, addressing a critical skills gap in the region.
Students gain hands-on experience with industry-standard security tools and platforms, and are supported in pursuing certifications such as CompTIA Security+, CEH, and CISSP.

note: Communication Arts programmes lead to the award of a Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree and are fully accredited by the Ghana Tertiary Education Commission (GTEC).
Entry requires credits in English Language, Core Mathematics, and Social Studies or Integrated Science, plus three relevant elective subjects with a WASSCE aggregate of 36 or better.
The faculty emphasises practical, studio-based learning with access to a professional media studio, newsroom simulation lab, and digital content creation facilities on campus.

Graduates are eligible for further studies at postgraduate level and for professional membership with bodies such as the Ghana Journalists Association (GJA) and the Institute of Public Relations Ghana (IPRG).

fees: Payment plan option: GHC 29,000 per semester in 3 instalments
mode: Full-time on-campus with newsroom lab and media studio practicals
Field reporting assignments, live broadcasts, and editorial workshops
Blended learning with online components and industry guest sessions
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credits (A1-C6) in English Language, Core Mathematics, and Social Studies or Integrated Science
Credits (A1-C6) in three relevant elective subjects (Arts, Languages, Humanities, or Business)
Overall aggregate of 36 or better
SSSCE equivalent: aggregate 24 or better; Level 200 entry available for HND holders in Journalism or Mass Communication
level_note: Academic City's Journalism & Mass Communication programme combines traditional media training with digital storytelling, social media, and multimedia production skills.
Students produce live broadcasts, publish articles in the university's student media outlet, and complete an industry internship at leading Ghanaian and international media houses.
